OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar May-24-2017 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT WANG & CHANG'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT (of Simon Chan)

Appearances: Andrea Loh of the Loh Legal Group (415.848.9095) for cross-defendant and moving party; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: the demurrer to the sole cause of action for professional negligence in Simon Chan's cross-complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Neither the face of the cross-complaint nor the judicially noticeable documents show that the professional negligence cause of action is time-barred. Moreover, it is likely that for statute of limitations purposes, the cross-complaint relates back to the filing of the complaint. (California-American Water Company v. Marina Coast Water District (2016) 2 Cal. App. 5th 748). However, Mr. Chan does not allege sufficient facts for his claim. It is unclear what actions or inactions constitute the allegedly negligent conduct and how it is more likely than not that Mr. Chan would have obtained a more favorable result but for the alleged negligence. If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Chan is granted leave to amend the complaint to cure these deficiencies. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Kelley DeYoung, CSR 6780 (kdeyoung@sftc.org) (302/HEK)

Notice Of Hearing For Entry Of Default Judgment By Court Against Defendant Simon Chan
Off Calendar Jan-12-2017 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-22-2016 Continued To Feb-22-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jan-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANDREA LOH OF THE LOH LEGAL GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-415-848-9095 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ANDREA G. WATTERS OF THE RAELLIC SYSTEMS, TEL.NO. 1-415-261-8527 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Simon Chan's motion to set aside entry of default is granted. Mr. Chan has shown sufficient grounds to set aside the default per CCP 473.5 and 473(b). Granting the motion is supported by California's strong public policy in favor of deciding cases on their merits and plaintiff is not unfairly prejudiced by the granting of the motion. Mr. Chan's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
